message:
Harden audio duration probing and accelerate benchmarking edge cases

Audio and accelerate module changes for robustness and test coverage:

audio.rs:
- Add guard in probe_duration_seconds_with_timeout to reject
  non-finite (NaN, Infinity) and negative duration values returned
  by ffprobe, returning None instead of propagating invalid values
  through the pipeline. This prevents downstream code from creating
  speculation windows with nonsensical durations.

accelerate.rs:
- Add BenchmarkHarness::new() empty harness report test verifying
  overall recommendation is UseCpu and GPU-unavailable note is emitted
- Add benchmark_result_from_timings exact boundary value tests for
  ratio == 1.1 (EitherFine, not UseGpu) and ratio == 0.9 (EitherFine,
  not UseCpu) documenting boundary-inclusive semantics
- Add recommend_backend all-EitherFine results test verifying
  aggregate recommendation preserves EitherFine when no lane wins
- Add jit_attention_block_cpu shortest-input-vector test verifying
  output length matches the shortest of q/k/v inputs with all
  values finite and non-negative
- Add jit_cache_key label-field independence test verifying that
  InferenceGraphSpec with different labels produces identical cache
  keys (labels are display-only, not part of cache identity)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
